mechanism_narrative: Persistently elevated nominal short-term interest rates increase the cost of capital and equity discount rates, reducing present valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return following months with above-median FEDFUNDS is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > 0 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating household income and consumption demand, triggering rapid downward revisions to corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the Rank IC between UNRATE change and 5-day returns is positive over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous combination of decelerating inflation and a stable or declining federal funds rate indicates a disinflationary easing environment that boosts real corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint regression of CPIAUCSL change and FEDFUNDS level fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of predicted cases, or the interaction term coefficient is not significant at the 5% level over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During consumer-led expansions, accelerating retail sales directly translate into higher expected nominal revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in less than 55% of months with above-median RSAFS growth, or Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening construction activity and associated durable goods demand, triggering downward revisions to cyclical earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 5 percent over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of episodes triggered by a 5% HOUST decline, or the signal's Rank IC is not negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth reflects rising operating leverage and capacity utilization, supporting margin expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Above-median INDPRO growth does not coincide with positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating inflation and a low nominal policy rate produces deeply negative real interest rates that compress equity risk premiums and foreshadow monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 4.0% while FEDFUNDS remains below 3.0%
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows no significant negative coefficient in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises do not coincide with positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of observations, or Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
